topic_title: Fine-tuning — adapting a foundation model on domain-specific data
verdict: All 8 checks pass. Reading is well-formed, within length budget, fully cites all resources (primary [1] cited at first appearance), faithful to corpus across 10 sampled assertions, free of forward-reference defects, beginner-accessible with concrete analogies before abstractions, well-formatted with no walls of prose, carries no assessment references, and stays within the fine-tuning topic boundary. Meets or exceeds the gold-example bar; notably the 4.2 reading avoids the assessment-reference defect present in the gold (3-4) example.

format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is H1 title. Sidecar reading.meta.json present and valid. Exactly the required 6 sections in correct order.

length.notes: 1421 words / 230 wpm = 6.18 min. Sidecar reports 7 min (using 200 wpm baseline = 7.1 min). Both within [6, 8].

citations.notes: Primary resource [1] (AWS SageMaker) cited at first inline use on line 11. All three markers have matching bibliography entries. No orphans.

no_forward_references.notes: References to '4.3–4.5' appear at lines 116 and 124 as bare mention-and-defer pointers ('covered in topics 4.3–4.5'), which is the permitted form. No forward-defined concepts taught.

faithfulness.notes: 10 assertions sampled across all sections: fine-tuning definition, pre-trained model definition, data quality claim, catastrophic forgetting definition, pre-training vs fine-tuning table, zero-shot use definition, epoch definition, overfitting definition, real-world scenarios (healthcare/legal/customer support/software), 'recalibrating an instrument' analogy. All trace to corpus sections 4–8.

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms defined at first use with bold-term / plain-meaning format. LLM spelled out 'LLM (Large Language Model)' at first use (line 82). API spelled out at line 94. No paragraph introduces 3+ brand-new terms cold. Analogies (generalist employee, recalibrating an instrument) precede or accompany abstractions. No hedging language detected. Sentences are concise and in plain register.

formatting.notes: No paragraph exceeds 6 sentences. Worked Example uses numbered steps (lines 80–94). Inline analogies appear in Key Concepts (lines 13–15, line 50) outside the Worked Example section. All definitions follow '**Term** — plain meaning' shape throughout.

no_assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due-week references, or grade weights found anywhere in the reading. Gold-example defect (assessment reference in Overview) is correctly absent here.

scope.notes: Content covers exactly: fine-tuning, pre-trained model, domain-specific data, labelled data, zero-shot use, catastrophic forgetting, hallucination, overfitting, epoch. Topics 4.3–4.5 (RAG, agents, tool use) appear only as a bare mention-and-defer pointer at lines 116 and 124, never bolded or defined. No out-of-scope material taught.

gold_example_comparison.notes: 4.2 reading matches gold on: six-section structure, H1-first (no frontmatter), bold-term definition shape, numbered steps in Worked Example, concrete analogy before abstraction, no jargon dumps, plain register, inline examples outside Worked Example. Exceeds gold on check 6b: the gold carries a flagged assessment-reference defect in Overview; the 4.2 reading correctly omits any such reference.
